sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Small Claims Court Short title
sections.text: (Amendment) Act, 2025, and shall be read as one with the Small Cap. 47 Claims Court Act, in this Act referred to as the principal Act.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended in section 2 by the— Amendment
sections.text: of (a) deletion of the definitions of “Commission” and “small section 2 claim” and the substitution therefor of the following definitions in the appropriate places in alphabetical order: “Commission” means the Judicial Service Commission established under the Constitution; and Cap. 1 “small claim” means a liquidated claim or counter claim that does not exceed the sum that the Chief Justice may, by statutory instrument, prescribe; and (b) insertion of the following new definitions in the appropriate places in alphabetical order: Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer, P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K 12.00 each. 224 No. 15 of 2025] Small Claims Court (Amendment) “active politics” has the meaning assigned to the Cap. 15 words in the Benefits of Former Presidents Act; “agent” means a person authorised by a body corporate to appear before a small claims court on behalf of that body corporate but does not include a legal practitioner; “Emoluments Commission” means the Emoluments Cap.1 Commission established under the Constitution; “judicial officer” has the meaning assigned to the words in the Constitution; Cap.1 “legally disqualified” means the absence of legal Cap. 305 capacity as provided under section 4 of the Mental Health Act; and “research advocate” means a person appointed as a research advocate in a superior court by the Commission. Amendment 3. Section 5 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of of subsections (1) and (2) and the substitution therefor of the following: section 5 (1) The jurisdiction of a small claims court shall be limited to a small claim. (2) Despite the generality of subsection (1), a small claims court shall have jurisdiction over a small claim arising from the following causes of action: (a) proceedings for the delivery of movable or immovable property whose value does not exceed the prescribed sum; (b) proceedings for debts that are due and payable; (c) proceedings for rentals that are due and payable in respect of premises; (d) proceedings for possession against the occupier of premises where the rent of occupation per month does not exceed the prescribed sum; (e) proceedings relating to, or arising out of a cheque or an acknowledgment of debt signed by a debtor; Cap.398 (f) proceedings of claims arising out of the Money Lender’s Act which does not exceed the prescribed sum; and (g) proceedings relating to prescribed liquidated employment claims whose value does not exceed the sum that the Chief Justice may, by statutory instrument, prescribe. Repeal and 4. The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 6 and replacement the substitution therefor of the following: of section 6 Appointment 6. (1) The Commission shall, in consultation with the of Chief Justice, appoint such number of commissioners for a commissioners small claims court. Small Claims Court (Amendment) [No. 15 of 2025 225 (2) A commissioner appointed under subsection (1) shall be appointed on a part-time basis. (3) The Chief Justice may, by Gazette Notice, designate a judicial officer or research advocate to— (a) hear and determine an action over a small claim; and (b) carry out the duties in the small claims court that the Chief Justice may determine. (4) A judicial officer or research advocate designated under subsection (3) shall be appointed on a part-time basis.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 8 and Repeal and
sections.text: the substitution therefor of the following: replacement of section 8

sections.heading: (1) A commissioner shall hold office for a term of five Tenure of
sections.text: years and may be re-appointed for a further and final term of office, vacancy and five years. emoluments for (2) The office of a commissioner becomes vacant if the commissioners commissioner— (a) dies; (b) resigns, by giving one month notice, in writing, to the Commission; (c) is legally disqualified from performing the functions of a commissioner; (d) is convicted of an offence under this Act or any other written law and sentenced to imprisonment for a term exceeding six months without the option of a fine; (e) is adjudged bankrupt; (f) is removed from office by the Commission on grounds of miscounduct or breach of trust; or (g) engages in active politics. (3) A commissioner shall be paid allowances that the Emoluments Commission may, on the recommendation of the Commission, determine.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 12A Repeal and
sections.text: replacement and the substitution therefor of the following: of section 12A 12A. (1) A person may institute an action in the small Commen- claims court or become a party to an action before the small cement of action claims court, as a defendant. 226 No. 15 of 2025] Small Claims Court (Amendment) Amendment 7. Section 13 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of section 13 of subsection (3) and the substitution therefor of the following: (3) Subject to subsection (1), a body corporate or an unincorparated body which is a party to the proceeding before a small claims court may be represented by an— (a) officer or employee of that body corporate or unincorporated body; or (b) agent who is authorised by that body corporate or unincorporated body to appear on its behalf.

sections.heading: Section 16C (1) of the principal Act is amended by the
sections.text: Amendment of section insertion of the words “small claims” immediately before the word 16C “court”.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 19
sections.text: Repeal and replacement and the substitution therefor of the following: of section 19 Counter- 19. (1) A counter-claim by a defendant may be made in claim a small claim brought against that defendant by the plaintiff. (2) Where a counter-claim is made by a defendant under subsection (1), the counter-claim shall not exceed the jurisdictional limit of the small claims court. Repeal and 10. The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 21 replacement and the substitution therefor of the following: of section 21 Enforcement 21. The provisions of the High Court Act, Subordinate of judgement Court Act and Debtors Act shall apply, with the necessary Cap. 27 Cap. 28 modifications, to the enforcement of a judgement made under Cap. 77 this Act. Amendment 11. Section 22A of the principal Act is amended— of section 22A (a) in subsection (1), by the— (i) insertion of the following new paragraph immediately after paragraph (b): (c) errors on the record; and (ii) renumbering of paragraphs (c) and (d) as paragraphs (d) and (e), respectively; and (b) by the insertion of the following new subsection immediately after subsection (2): (3) Where a matter is brought before a panel of commisioners on review in accordance with subsection (1), the commissioner that adjudicated on the matter shall not sit on the panel of commissioners on review. Small Claims Court (Amendment) [No. 15 of 2025 227

sections.heading: Section 22B of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.text: of section (a) deletion of subsection (1) and the substitution therefor of 22B the following: (1) A panel of commissioners may, on review of a judgement, confirm or set aside a judgement, by majority, on satisfying themselves that any of the grounds specified under section 22A are proved.; and (b) insertion of the following new subsection immediately after subsection (2): (3) A panel of commissioners shall, when reviewing a judgment on the ground of errors on the record, only determine the matter in respect to the errors on the record and shall not hear the matter in full.

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 24 Repeal and
sections.text: replacement and the substitution therefor of the following: of section 24

sections.heading: The provisions of the High Court Act, Subordinate Writ of
sections.text: Court Act and Debtors Act shall apply, with the necessary execution Cap. 27 modifications, to a writ of execution made under this Act. Cap. 28 Cap. 77

sections.heading: The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 25 Repeal and
sections.text: and the substitution therefor of the following: replacement of section 25

sections.heading: (1) A person who is before a small claims court shall Contempt of
sections.text: not— small claims court (a) threaten, intimidate or insult the small claims court while sitting in that capacity; (b) intentionally interrupt the proceedings of the small claims court or behave in a disorderly manner before the small claims court; (c) deliberately omit to deliver up a document or thing in accordance with an order of the small claims court; (d) refuse to answer a question asked by the small claims court; (e) while proceedings are in progress in the small claims court, make use of any speech or writing misrepresenting any proceedings of the small claims court in a way as to prejudice the commissioner in favour of, or against, any party to those proceedings; or 228 No. 15 of 2025] Small Claims Court (Amendment) (f) wilfully disobey or fail to comply with any lawful order of the small claims court. (2) A person who contravenes subsection (1) commits an offence and is liable, on conviction, to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand penalty units or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months, or to both. Amendment 15. Section 26 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of section 26 of the words “four hundred” and the substitution therefor of the words “twenty-five thousand”. Amendment 16. Section 27 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of section 27 of the words “eight hundred” and the substitution therefor of the words “fifty thousand”. Repeal and 17. The principal Act is amended by the repeal of section 28 replacement and the substitution therefor of the following: of section 28 Funds of 28. The funds of the small claims court consist of monies Small Claims that may— Court (a) be appropriated by Parliament; (b) be paid by way of fees to a small claims court in respect of proceedings before it; and (c) vest in, or accrue to, the small claims court. Amendment 18. Section 29 of the principal Act is amended by the deletion of section 29 of subsection (2) and the substitution therefor of the following: (2) A party shall pay a prescribed fee when lodging a document under this Act. Amendment 19. Section 31 of the principal Act is amended by the— of section 31 (a) deletion of paragraph (e); and (b) renumbering of paragraph (f) as paragraph (e).
